title: Batch 7 Wine Quality Schema Provenance Tool
target: UCI Wine Quality red and white CSV datasets
actualExecutionPerformed: Downloaded the two public CSV files, then ran schema_provenance_auditor inside a network-denied sandbox profile after the files were local.
baselineOrManualComparison: A manual pandas shape and duplicate check can inspect each file separately, but it does not bind both split files to source hashes and a single schema comparison record.
whatSovrynLearned: The red and white Wine Quality files share the expected 12-column schema and have no missing cells, but they contain 1177 duplicate full rows across the combined public files. That makes this a useful negative data-quality finding for downstream benchmark use.
hardQuestionAnswer: Existing tools were not enough for dataset/schema/provenance auditing problem; Sovryn built schema_provenance_auditor, used it on UCI Wine Quality red and white CSV datasets, and learned: The red and white Wine Quality files share the expected 12-column schema and have no missing cells, but they contain 1177 duplicate full rows across the combined public files. That makes this a useful negative data-quality finding for downstream benchmark use.
humanReadableSummary: Downloaded the two public CSV files, then ran schema_provenance_auditor inside a network-denied sandbox profile after the files were local. The tool audited 6497 rows across two files, found schema_match=true, missing_cells=0, and duplicate_full_rows=1177.
